A strong opinion is not enough. I need a strong argument.

Patricia, your case is interesting, but why should we fund phase two now?
Because the trial gives us strong proof. The data demonstrate a clear impact on glycemic control.
And what is the main factor behind your recommendation?
The main factor is the consistency of the results, therefore a larger budget would lead to a larger study.
That is a convincing rationale. Add one slide on the budget, and we can approve it.
Wonderful. To conclude, the proof is clear, and as a result I am confident we should invest.
The data demonstrate a clear impact.

My rationale is simple, therefore I recommend phase two.
To conclude, the main factor is the consistent proof.

Good afternoon, everyone, and thank you for your time. Today I want to make the case for expanding our functional ingredients program. Let me start with the proof. In our last trial, patients who used the new formula saw a clear improvement in glycemic control. The data points are consistent across three different groups, and that is the main factor behind my recommendation. Because the results are so strong, I believe the impact on patient care could be significant. The numbers demonstrate that the formula works, and therefore I am confident we should move forward. Of course, a larger budget would lead to a larger study, and a larger study would give us even more reliable results. My rationale is simple. We have the proof, we understand the impact, and as a result we should invest now. To conclude, I recommend that we approve phase two of the project before the end of the quarter. Thank you, and I am happy to answer your questions.

Hi Patricia, this is David from the steering committee. I listened to your pitch about expanding the functional ingredients program, and overall I found your argument very convincing. Your rationale is clear, and the proof from the last trial is strong. The data points really demonstrate the impact on glycemic control, and that is an important factor for the committee. Because your case is so well structured, I am inclined to support phase two. That said, I have one request. Could you add a short slide that shows how the bigger budget would lead to a bigger sample? If you can demonstrate that link clearly, I think the committee will conclude that the investment makes sense. As a result, we could approve the project faster. Send me the updated version by Thursday, and therefore we will be ready for the vote on Friday. Great work, and talk soon.

First, state your recommendation clearly. Next, present the proof and the main data points. After that, explain the cause and effect: because of the results, this is the impact. Then, give your rationale in one short sentence. Finally, conclude and ask for a decision.
